question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: mRNA takes genetic information out of the cell.tRNA brings amino acids with complementary codons to attach      to the mRNA strand.tRNA balances the chemical amounts that come with the amino acids.A protein is created with the new nucleotide sequence.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: a) A conclusion about this data is that plastic type A+B stretched longer the second trial and that type C stretched longer the first and D stayed the same. You can also conclude that plastic B has greatest stretchability. B) Two ways [the students could have improved the experimental are sign is that they could have done more trials to increase validity. They also could have used the same length of different plastics each time.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: From the student's experiment I can conclude that plastic type B was able to stretch the most while plastic type A stretched the least. However, these students need to improve their experimental design. Two ways that is possible would be to give definite and constant stretching results for each sample as well as defining how much weight should be used and where the clamp should be placed exactly. This would definitely improve the validity of their results.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.

Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: Passive transportationActive transportationDiffusion

question: Organization of Article item: The article 'Orbiting Junk' explains the problem of space junk. It begins by introducing space debris around Earth, then explains what space junk is, gives examples of how debris is created through dead satellites, collisions, and lost objects such as a tool bag, and finally explains why even small pieces of debris are dangerous and difficult to manage. Explain how the author organizes the article. Support your response with details from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: the author organizes the article by first introducing the problem of space junk, then explaining what space junk is and how it is created, then giving examples such as dead satellites colliding and an astronaut losing a tool bag, and finally explaining why small pieces of debris are dangerous and why the problem is difficult to solve. A strong response should identify the article's organizational structure and support it with relevant article details. Scoring guide: score 2 = clear, text-based explanation of the article's organization with relevant details; score 1 = partial, general, simplistic, or weakly supported explanation; score 0 = inaccurate, incomplete, missing, unsupported, or irrelevant response.
student_answer: The author organizes the article very well. First he tells about what it actually is, then explains that it is a serious problem and is dangerous to astronauts and our Earth. Then supports and backs up any information withh stastitics.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.

question: Koala/Panda item: The article contrasts specialist and generalist species. It explains that pandas in China eat almost nothing but bamboo and koalas in Australia eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively, making both animals specialists. It also presents pythons as generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Explain how pandas are similar to koalas and how both are different from pythons. Support your response with information from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: pandas and koalas are similar because both are specialists with narrow food needs; pandas eat almost nothing but bamboo, and koalas eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively. Both are different from pythons because pythons are generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. question: Doghouse item: Brandi and Jerry tested how lid color affects the air temperature inside a glass jar exposed to a lamp. Choose one paint color for the doghouse, describe how that color might affect the temperature inside the doghouse, and use results from the experiment to support the description.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 2 = chooses a paint color, describes how that color might affect the inside of the doghouse, and uses experimental results to support the description; score 1 = chooses a color and describes the temperature effect but does not use experimental results, or chooses a color and uses experimental results but does not describe the temperature effect; score 0 = little or no understanding. Valid supported responses include: black would make the doghouse warmer because the black lid made the jar warmest; dark gray would make the inside a little warmer but not too hot because the dark gray lid increased 6??C more than the white; light gray would keep the inside cooler but not too cool because the light gray lid was 8??C cooler than the black; white would keep the inside cooler because the white lid only went up to 42??C.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: tRNAgRNA

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: A conclusion I can draw, based on the start ends data, is that plastic type B had stretched the most at the end of both trials. Also, I can draw the conclusion that type A stretched the least after both trials. 2 ways the students could have improved the experiment or validity of the results are to control variables and perform more trials. They could have improved the experiment by controlling the number of weights, type of clamp, and the height that the plastic hung from.

Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: tRNA

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: One process that a cell uses to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane is the surface area. If the surface area is too large, the cell won't let any more substances move throughout the cell membrane. Another way is facilitated diffusion. This controls the amount of substances that are allowed to enter the cell.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.

question: Organization of Article item: The article 'Orbiting Junk' explains the problem of space junk. It begins by introducing space debris around Earth, then explains what space junk is, gives examples of how debris is created through dead satellites, collisions, and lost objects such as a tool bag, and finally explains why even small pieces of debris are dangerous and difficult to manage. Explain how the author organizes the article. Support your response with details from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: the author organizes the article by first introducing the problem of space junk, then explaining what space junk is and how it is created, then giving examples such as dead satellites colliding and an astronaut losing a tool bag, and finally explaining why small pieces of debris are dangerous and why the problem is difficult to solve. A strong response should identify the article's organizational structure and support it with relevant article details. Scoring guide: score 2 = clear, text-based explanation of the article's organization with relevant details; score 1 = partial, general, simplistic, or weakly supported explanation; score 0 = inaccurate, incomplete, missing, unsupported, or irrelevant response.
student_answer: The author begins the article with an attention grabber. The author does this in order to place excitement into the article straight from the beginning. Then the author asks the question, 'What is space junk?' and follows it with a historical answer. The author goes into detail about the amount of spacecraft, including space capsules, telescopes, and satelites, that have been launched into space over the last 52 years.     With all of this stuff in orbit around the earth, many things can crash and become space debris. This poses a threat to future space missions because bits of junk flying at intense speeds can cause harm to a spacecraft or an astronaut. After explaining the possible harms of space junk, the author starts to explain that NASA (National Aeronautics and Space Agency) is trying its best to find a solution to this problem, but has not found one yet.

Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: First mRNA leaves the nucleus with a strand of DNA which contains the instructions to making an amino acid. It takes the DNA strand to the tRNA in the ribosomes. There the tRNA uses the codons to make the sequence of amino acids. The amino acids are then transferred by the tRNA to the rRNA where they become a protein.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.

question: Mr. Leonard item: In the story, Paul learns background information about Mr. Leonard: Mr. Leonard had been a talented college hurdler who won the 400-meter hurdles at nationals, but he struggled academically, lost his scholarship, and left college because he could not read well. Explain the effect this background information has on Paul. Support your response with details from the story.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: the background information about Mr. Leonard affects Paul by helping him understand that Mr. Leonard has faced struggles similar to his own reading difficulties. Paul realizes that Mr. Leonard helped him become a better runner despite Mr. Leonard's own painful experience with school and lost opportunity. This makes Paul feel empathy and motivates him to help Mr. Leonard in return, shown when Paul says it is time to start Mr. Leonard's training. Scoring guide: score 2 = clear explanation of the effect on Paul with relevant story details; score 1 = partial or general explanation with limited, overly simple, or weakly supported details; score 0 = inaccurate, incomplete, missing, unsupported, or irrelevant response.
student_answer: The narrator is surprised at the information. He never expected that Mr. Leonard was a track star.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: Diffusion- Sugar is absorbed into the membrane and travels down the gradient level.Faciliated Diffusion- Requires energy to move down the gradient.Active Transport- Particles are held in a 'pocket', then are brought to the membrane and are released out of the cell.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.

question: Invasive Species item: The article discusses nonnative reptiles, especially Burmese pythons in Florida, and presents two views: MacInnes argues that the term 'invasive' is unfair and judgmental, while biologists argue that invasive species can spread without natural predators and threaten biodiversity. Explain the significance of the word 'invasive' to the rest of the article. Support your response with information from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: the word 'invasive' is significant because it frames nonnative species as harmful, spreading, and disruptive rather than merely introduced. In the article, pythons and other reptiles are described as threats to biodiversity because they can spread without natural predators and endanger native species. A strong response may also recognize the contrast between MacInnes's view that the word is judgmental and the biologists' view that invasive species are a serious ecological threat. Scoring guide: score 2 = thoughtful and thorough examination of the text with relevant support; score 1 = sufficient but limited examination with some relevant support; score 0 = cursory, vague, unsupported, inaccurate, or irrelevant response.

Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: Cell growth, cell division, replication.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: The mRNA leaves the nucleas.  It then captures a protein.  It brings the protein to this place where DNA is made.  It uses the protein to make the DNA.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: One process is that the cells use their functions to move substances. Two is that differnet organelles help the cells move the substances. And the third is that the cells guide the substances to their destination.

question: Koala/Panda item: The article contrasts specialist and generalist species. It explains that pandas in China eat almost nothing but bamboo and koalas in Australia eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively, making both animals specialists. It also presents pythons as generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Explain how pandas are similar to koalas and how both are different from pythons. Support your response with information from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: pandas and koalas are similar because both are specialists with narrow food needs; pandas eat almost nothing but bamboo, and koalas eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively. Both are different from pythons because pythons are generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Scoring guide: score 2 = strong interpretation with relevant article evidence; score 1 = partial or limited interpretation with some relevant evidence; score 0 = vague, irrelevant, unsupported, or inaccurate response.
student_answer: Pandas in China are similar to koalas in Australia in that they are both specialist species, meaning they need very specific conditions, climate and habitat to flourish. As the text says pandas eat "almost nothing but bamboo" and the koala "eats eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively." On the other hand, pythons are classified as a generalist species, able to live in a wider range of habitats and live off of a wider range of food sources , whether in Asia of the U.S. The main difference here is ability to survive in our quickly changing world: the generalists (python) hold a huge advantage over the specialists (panda and koala.)

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: Plastic type A stretches longer than the other plastic types. The student could improve the experiment by putting the name of plastics. And add another trial. 3 trials are always best.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: mRNA, tRNA, rRNA

question: Doghouse item: Brandi and Jerry tested how lid color affects the air temperature inside a glass jar exposed to a lamp. They tested black, dark gray, light gray, and white lids, starting each trial at 24??C and measuring the temperature after 10 minutes. They were designing a doghouse and needed to choose a paint color. Choose one paint color for the doghouse, describe how that color might affect the temperature inside the doghouse, and use results from the experiment to support the description.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 2 = chooses a paint color, describes how that color might affect the inside of the doghouse, and uses experimental results to support the description; score 1 = chooses a color and describes the temperature effect but does not use experimental results, or chooses a color and uses experimental results but does not describe the temperature effect; score 0 = little or no understanding. Valid supported responses include: black would make the doghouse warmer because the black lid made the jar warmest; dark gray would make the inside a little warmer but not too hot because the dark gray lid increased 6??C more than the white; light gray would keep the inside cooler but not too cool because the light gray lid was 8??C cooler than the black; white would keep the inside cooler because the white lid only went up to 42??C.
student_answer: light gray ::  Dark grey would affect the temperature in the dog house by making it so that the dog house was neither too hot or too cold. The results of the experiment show that the air inside of the jar with the white lid had a average tempeture of about 42(DEG)C. In the winter if the dog house was painted white it would get to cold because the experiment shows that white does not retain heat well. Black was the color that retained the most heat in the experiment with an average tempeture of 53(DEG)C. However, even though according to the experiment,the dog house would be warm in the winter if the doghouse was painted black it would be too hot in the summer for the dog to remain cool. The experiment shows that the air inside of the jar with the light grey lid was warmer than the jar with the white lid, but only by 3(DEG)C, therefore the dog house should be painted dark grey because in the experiment the jar with the dark grey lid is 5(DEG)C cooler than the jar with the black lid. The dark grey would allow the doghouse to remain cool in the summer, but remain warm in the winter.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: During mRNA, it leaves the nucleus and goes into the blood stream. Once it is in the blood stream, it goes through and goes to the mitochondria where the sister cells replicate. After they replicate one set of the genetic information goes back into the blood stream. Then it goes to the brain. This action is completed in just seconds.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: Active transport uses energy and goes against the gradient. Diffusion moves water from a high surface area to a low surface area. Osmosis also moves water from a high surface area to a low surface area.

Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: RNA leaves the nucleus. Then it brings back protien

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: Out of the four different polymer plastics for stretchability the most stretched was plastic type b. The least amount stretched was plastic a. Two ways the students could have improved the experimental design and/ or validity of the results would have tried different time lengths. maybe like 10 minutes after 5 minutes. Repeat the experiment all together with all types of plastics.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: Active transport- uses energy to move subatances across the membranePassive transport- does not use energy to move substances across the membraneOsmosis- the diffusion of water across the membrane

question: Invasive Species item: The article discusses nonnative reptiles, especially Burmese pythons in Florida, and presents two views: MacInnes argues that the term 'invasive' is unfair and judgmental, while biologists argue that invasive species can spread without natural predators and threaten biodiversity. Explain the significance of the word 'invasive' to the rest of the article. Support your response with information from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: the word 'invasive' is significant because it frames nonnative species as harmful, spreading, and disruptive rather than merely introduced. In the article, pythons and other reptiles are described as threats to biodiversity because they can spread without natural predators and endanger native species. A strong response may also recognize the contrast between MacInnes's view that the word is judgmental and the biologists' view that invasive species are a serious ecological threat. Scoring guide: score 2 = thoughtful and thorough examination of the text with relevant support; score 1 = sufficient but limited examination with some relevant support; score 0 = cursory, vague, unsupported, inaccurate, or irrelevant response.

question: Koala/Panda item: The article contrasts specialist and generalist species. It explains that pandas in China eat almost nothing but bamboo and koalas in Australia eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively, making both animals specialists. It also presents pythons as generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Explain how pandas are similar to koalas and how both are different from pythons. Support your response with information from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: pandas and koalas are similar because both are specialists with narrow food needs; pandas eat almost nothing but bamboo, and koalas eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively. Both are different from pythons because pythons are generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Scoring guide: score 2 = strong interpretation with relevant article evidence; score 1 = partial or limited interpretation with some relevant evidence; score 0 = vague, irrelevant, unsupported, or inaccurate response.

The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: Based on the results of the experiment, plastic type B had the least overall stretch. Although the groups results are not valid. There are two ways to improve the experiment in an experiment, you should always construct 3 trials to validate your results also, all of the samples should be exactly the same length.

question: Koala/Panda item: The article contrasts specialist and generalist species. It explains that pandas in China eat almost nothing but bamboo and koalas in Australia eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively, making both animals specialists. It also presents pythons as generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Explain how pandas are similar to koalas and how both are different from pythons. Support your response with information from the article.
reference_answer: Expected interpretation: pandas and koalas are similar because both are specialists with narrow food needs; pandas eat almost nothing but bamboo, and koalas eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively. Both are different from pythons because pythons are generalists or invasive animals that can survive and spread in broader or changing environments. Scoring guide: score 2 = strong interpretation with relevant article evidence; score 1 = partial or limited interpretation with some relevant evidence; score 0 = vague, irrelevant, unsupported, or inaccurate response.
student_answer: Pandas are similar to koalas because they both eat eucalyptus leaves almost exclusively as it states in the article. They are both different from pythons because they are not reptiles.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: One process used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane is osmosis. Cells use osmosis to either gain or get rid of water by passing it across the cell membrane.      Another process used by cells is cellular diffusion. This is like osmosis, it is the process of substances moving from an area of high concentration, to an area of low concentration.

A strong response should identify the article's organizational structure and support it with relevant article details. question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: MEOSIS,MIEOSIS,MUTATION

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: The conclusion that was drawn was that plastic type (A) had the longest length after being stretched with weights. The students could have improved the experiment by having more than two trials, and also by being more specific about the amount of weights that were used.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: The mRNA goes to the ribosomes for the instructions on making protein.  It then goes to the mitochondria to get energy. They it goes back to the nucleus to produce the protein.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: endocytosis- cell membrane envelopes material and is absorbed into the cell.diffusion- concentration gradient is higher outside the cell causing the material to naturally diffuse across the membrane.facilitated diffusion- carrier proteins help materials move with the concentration gradient through the cell membrane.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: 1. Active transport is a process that requires energy because it moves substances against their concentration gradient.2. Simple diffusion is a type of passive transport that moves substances down their concentration gradient. This process requires no energy.3. Facilitated diffusion is a type of passive transport that uses protein to move substances across the cell membrane. No energy is required for this process.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: a) Plastic type B and D are better to use because they stretch the stretchiest. Plastic B can stretch up to 20mm at most while plastic D can stretch up to 23 mm at most. Plastic C and plastic A can stretch the least because plastic C can stretch up to 14mm at most while plastic A can stretch up to 12 mm at most. ^p b) Two ways they would have improved the experiment is by adding more trials and telling what each plastic actually was.

question: Polymer Investigation item: A student tested four plastic samples for stretchability. Each sample was measured, hung from a table with a clamp and weights for five minutes, then measured again. The experiment was repeated for two trials. The amount stretched was: Plastic A = 10 mm and 12 mm; Plastic B = 22 mm and 23 mm; Plastic C = 14 mm and 13 mm; Plastic D = 20 mm and 20 mm. Based on these data, draw a conclusion about the stretchability of the plastics and describe two ways to improve the experimental design or the validity of the results.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = a valid conclusion supported by the data and two valid experimental design improvements; score 2 = a valid conclusion and one valid improvement, or two valid improvements without a correct conclusion; score 1 = a valid conclusion only, or one valid improvement only; score 0 = little or no correct information. Valid conclusions include: Plastic B has the most stretchability; Plastic A has the least stretchability; not all polymer plastics have the same stretchability; different plastics have different stretchability; or a reasonable conclusion cannot be drawn due to procedural errors. Valid improvements include: provide before and after length measurements; make sure all samples have the same thickness; perform additional trials; indicate how many weights were added to the clamps.
student_answer: Plastic type B streched the most at 45mm over the two trials, and Plastic A streched the least at 22mm. ^p The student could have improved the experiment by doing more trials and specify what kind of plastics they are using.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: mRNA exits the nucleus via a nuclear pore; mRNA travels through the cytoplasm to the ribosome or enters the rough endoplasmic reticulum; mRNA bases are read in triplets called codons; tRNA carrying the complementary anticodon recognizes the complementary codon of the mRNA; corresponding amino acids on tRNA are bonded to adjacent tRNA amino acids; a new corresponding amino acid is added to the tRNA; amino acids are linked together to make a protein beginning with a START codon in the P site; amino acids continue to be linked until a STOP codon is read on the mRNA in the A site.
student_answer: First, the mRNA leaves the nucleus and a ribosome attatches to it. Second, the ribosome reads every three nucleotide sequence, or codon, and calls for a tRNA molecule that has an anticodon to compliment the codon. Third, after this process is repeated, the amino acids on the tRNA molecules form peptide bonds with each other, forming a protein. Finally, after the mRNA is translated until a stop codon, the protein is released and used in the cell as needed.

question: Cell Membrane item: List and describe three processes used by cells to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = three valid key elements; score 2 = two valid key elements; score 1 = one valid key element; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. Valid key elements: selective permeability allows certain substances to move across the cell membrane; passive transport moves substances from higher concentration to lower concentration; osmosis is the diffusion of water across the cell membrane; facilitated diffusion occurs when the membrane controls the pathway for a particle to enter or leave a cell; active transport uses energy to move a substance across the membrane or from low to high concentration or against the concentration gradient; pumps move charged particles such as sodium and potassium ions through membranes using energy and carrier proteins; exocytosis occurs when a vesicle fuses with the cell membrane and forces large molecules out of the cell; endocytosis occurs when molecules are engulfed by the cell membrane; phagocytosis occurs when vesicles form around large molecules; pinocytosis occurs when vesicles form around liquid droplets; protein channels or channel proteins allow specific molecules or substances to move into or out of the cell.
student_answer: There are three different types of processes a cell uses to control the movement of substances across the cell membrane. The first type of movement is called active transport, which is when the cell is physically doing something. The second type of movement is called passive transport, which is when the cell itself if inactive and the person is doing the work. The third type of movement is osmosis which is the diffusion of water.

question: Protein Synthesis item: Starting with mRNA leaving the nucleus, list and describe four major steps involved in protein synthesis.
reference_answer: Scoring rule: score 3 = four valid key elements; score 2 = three valid key elements; score 1 = one or two valid key elements; score 0 = other, irrelevant, or incorrect response. The article reads, 'Yet the problem is certain to persist. In fact, the amount of space trash is actually increasing because commercial space travel is on the rise and more nations have undertaken space exploration.' The persuasive techniques he uses leaves us concerned and wondering about how space pollution could affect us.
